authoritative_sources.title: Best practices for Amazon RDS
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS official RDS documentation directly governs RDS operational guidance, including monitoring, storage headroom, automated backups, I/O capacity, DNS failover behavior, failover testing, and engine lifecycle constraints.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: platform intake, option assessment, operations troubleshooting, failover readiness, completion validation

authoritative_sources.title: Introduction to backups
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS official backup documentation establishes RDS automated backup, backup retention, point-in-time recovery, and restore workflow assumptions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: backup runbook, restore feasibility review, completion evidence capture

authoritative_sources.title: AWS Well-Architected Framework: Reliability Pillar
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS official reliability framework supports failure domain, quota, backup, disaster recovery, recovery-objective, and recovery-test expectations for cloud database platforms.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: resilience assessment, DR planning, recovery validation, exception escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Availability through local and zone redundancy - Azure SQL Database
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft official Azure SQL Database documentation defines local and zone redundancy behavior, service tier implications, and availability assumptions for Azure database platform operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Azure platform pattern selection, failover ADR, residency and redundancy review

authoritative_sources.title: Automated backups in Azure SQL Database
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft official backup documentation governs Azure SQL automatic backup, point-in-time restore, long-term retention, and backup redundancy assumptions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: backup policy review, restore runbook, recovery evidence

authoritative_sources.title: Azure Well-Architected Framework: Reliability
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft official reliability framework supports failure mode analysis, reliability targets, redundancy, recovery strategy, self-healing, and drills for Azure-hosted database workloads.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: resilience planning, reliability tradeoffs, recovery drill evidence

authoritative_sources.title: About high availability - Cloud SQL for MySQL
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Google Cloud official Cloud SQL documentation defines high availability configuration, failover prerequisites, failover conditions, and instance behavior for Cloud SQL database platforms.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Google Cloud platform pattern selection, failover ADR, operational readiness

authoritative_sources.title: Cloud SQL backups overview
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Google Cloud official backup documentation governs Cloud SQL backup policies, retained backups, recovery use, and backup lifecycle assumptions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: backup policy review, restore runbook, completion evidence
practical_use_in_pack: Cloud SQL backup and restore constraints

authoritative_sources.title: Google Cloud Well-Architected Framework: Reliability pillar
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Google Cloud official reliability framework supports realistic reliability goals, redundancy, observability, graceful degradation, recovery testing, data-loss recovery testing, and postmortem practices.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: reliability target framing, DR testing, observability readiness, post-incident evidence

authoritative_sources.title: StatefulSet
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Kubernetes official documentation defines stateful workload identity, ordered rollout, stable storage, and persistent volume retention caveats relevant to cloud-native database platform operation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Kubernetes-backed stateful database assessment, rollout, rollback, adjacent routing

authoritative_sources.title: Storage Classes
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Kubernetes official storage documentation defines storage class behavior, reclaim policy, volume expansion, defaulting, topology constraints, and provisioning assumptions affecting database durability and recovery.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: storage class review, durability assessment, rollback and deletion-risk review

authoritative_sources.title: Site Reliability Engineering Workbook: Incident Response
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Google-authored SRE reference provides recognized workflow guidance for incident roles, communication, mitigation, working records, escalation, and post-incident evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: incident checklist, severity mapping, escalation and evidence capture

authoritative_sources.title: Site Reliability Engineering Workbook: Monitoring
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Google-authored SRE reference provides recognized monitoring guidance for alert design, user-impact signals, diagnosis support, and operational telemetry expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: observability intake, alert design, troubleshooting, incident evidence
practical_use_in_pack: replica lag and storage alert expectations

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5, Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST official control catalog is a recognized public control source for contingency planning, system backup, recovery, audit, incident response, and configuration-management evidence expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: control evidence review, backup and recovery evidence, completion evidence capture

authoritative_sources.title: HashiCorp Terraform provider resource documentation for database resources
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HashiCorp provider registry is the authoritative reference for Terraform-managed cloud database resource schema and configuration arguments; runtime use must verify tenant provider and version.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: IaC checkpoint review, provider-version evidence, deployment guardrail review

workflow_stages.name: Scope screening
workflow_stages.description: Classify whether the request is database platform operations, adjacent specialist work, low-confidence, or outside allowed authority.

workflow_stages.name: Tenant evidence intake
workflow_stages.description: Collect workload, RTO/RPO, topology, data classification, backup, restore, monitoring, IaC, incident, change, policy, and owner records.

workflow_stages.name: Platform option assessment
workflow_stages.description: Compare managed database, zone-redundant, cross-zone, read-replica, self-managed Kubernetes, backup, storage, and scaling options against tenant goals.

workflow_stages.name: Runbook or decision artifact production
workflow_stages.description: Produce ADRs, runbooks, incident checklists, migration plans, configuration checkpoints, or tradeoff summaries with source and evidence traceability.

workflow_stages.name: Exception or handoff
workflow_stages.description: Queue exceptions or return to orchestrator when records, approvals, source precedence, authority, or adjacent ownership are unresolved.

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence capture
workflow_stages.description: Record case disposition, artifacts produced, evidence references, validation status, unresolved risks, exceptions, and next owner.

artifacts: shortlist of cloud-native database platform patterns with tradeoffs
resiliency and failover architecture decision record
IaC and configuration checkpoints for deployment
incident response checklist mapped to severity
systems_of_record: cloud provider account, subscription, project, region, zone, and service inventory
managed database service records for AWS RDS/Aurora, Azure SQL and Azure Database services, Google Cloud SQL/AlloyDB/Spanner where relevant, and tenant-approved Kubernetes stateful platforms
backup vaults, backup policies, recovery-point catalogs, restore drill records, and disaster-recovery evidence
monitoring, logging, alerting, dashboard, incident, postmortem, and on-call records
IaC repositories, Terraform state and provider lock files, CI/CD plans, policy-as-code results, and deployment logs
CMDB, service catalog, architecture decision records, dependency maps, change records, exception registers, and approval matrices
tenant policy, contractual, privacy, residency, retention, encryption, and compliance evidence stores

allowed: Recommend database platform architecture options and tradeoffs.
Draft backup, restore, failover, migration, incident, and completion-evidence runbooks.
Review platform-level capacity, storage, scaling, observability, and IaC guardrails.
State source-backed assumptions, missing evidence, validation gates, risks, and owner handoffs.
Route adjacent or low-confidence work to the orchestrator.
escalate: Required tenant facts, systems of record, source currency, or authority context are missing.
Public sources, tenant policy, contractual rules, jurisdictional constraints, or owner instructions conflict.

Execution requires production change approval, failover execution, restoration, data migration, risk acceptance, spend commitment, privacy/legal interpretation, compliance attestation, or incident command.
Application, security, identity, network, storage administration, DBA, legal, procurement, finance, or incident-command ownership becomes primary.
refuse: Application SQL model design, schema migration scripts, query rewrites, ORM configuration, API-layer data-access architecture, or application code refactoring.
Identity-only governance, endpoint hardening, malware forensics, credential-abuse investigation, breach response, or regulated notification duties.
Vendor/contract negotiation, exact pricing quotes, procurement, legal advice, compliance attestation, or risk acceptance.
Generic best-practice output that lacks named provider, database, backup, restore, monitoring, IaC, change, incident, or policy records.

domain_failure_modes: RTO/RPO stated without restore-test evidence or recovery-point records.
Single-AZ or single-zone architecture treated as highly available without accepted risk.
Provider-specific HA, backup, or failover behavior generalized across AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, or Kubernetes without source support.
Replica lag, storage saturation, DNS failover, or client reconnect behavior omitted from incident and failover plans.
Backup retention, deletion protection, encryption, maintenance window, monitoring, or alerting guardrails missing from IaC.
Kubernetes stateful storage reclaim, topology, expansion, or StatefulSet rollout assumptions ignored.
Tenant residency, privacy, contractual, or retention policy conflicts hidden under generic cloud guidance.
Application query tuning or schema ownership absorbed by platform operations.
Security incident, credential abuse, legal breach response, or customer notification ownership absorbed by platform operations.
Completion claimed without artifacts, evidence links, validation results, exception notes, or next-owner handoff.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Normal in-scope execution from accepted work to completion
Processing blocked by missing upstream data or approvals
Tenant-specific adaptation with public baseline preserved

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Provider feature and Terraform argument currency
unresolved_ambiguities.ambiguity: Cloud database features, service tiers, limits, and Terraform provider arguments can change faster than the spec pack release cycle.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Runtime materializers must verify current official provider and Terraform documentation before asserting feature availability or configuration syntax.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific legal, privacy, residency, and contractual precedence
unresolved_ambiguities.ambiguity: Public cloud guidance may permit patterns that tenant policy, contract, jurisdiction, or data classification forbids.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The specialist records the conflict and returns to orchestrator or authorized legal/compliance/privacy owner for precedence instead of synthesizing a final rule.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Self-managed database versus Kubernetes platform operator boundary
unresolved_ambiguities.ambiguity: Some Kubernetes-hosted database requests may belong primarily to Kubernetes platform operations or DBA administration rather than this lane.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The specialist keeps database platform reliability and evidence guidance in scope and routes operator implementation, application workload tuning, or DBA-specific tasks to adjacent specialists.

research_questions_answered.question: What named-industry records and systems actually govern the lane?
research_questions_answered.answer: Cloud provider database service records, backup and restore records, monitoring and incident systems, IaC repositories and state, change records, CMDB/service catalogs, policy evidence stores, and approval matrices govern the lane.

research_questions_answered.question: What failure modes make this industry materially different from a generic function?
research_questions_answered.answer: Database platform operations hinges on RTO/RPO, backup restore evidence, failover semantics, storage durability, replica lag, provider-specific HA, data residency, retention, and irreversible production data movement risks.

research_questions_answered.question: What adjacent authority boundaries constrain the lane?
research_questions_answered.answer: Application schema/query/code ownership, DBA administration, Kubernetes operator implementation, identity/security operations, legal/compliance/privacy interpretation, procurement/FinOps approval, incident command, and production execution constrain the lane.

market_ready_research_conclusion: The public source set covers provider operating guidance, workflow references, and evidence/control standards. No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because runtime-specific uncertainty is handled through source-refresh checks, tenant evidence requirements, and orchestrator-return rules.
